cases.prompt: Set up Pixy for a 32x32 game character sprite with a transparent background, then make me a slime sprite.

assertions.text: A pixy.spec.json is created with a 32x32 canvas and a transparent background before any sprite is drawn.
The agent confirmed use-case, size, transparency, and palette with the user (or used the matching preset) rather than guessing silently.
A transparent-background PNG is rendered from a .pix grid via render_sprite.py at the spec dimensions.

cases.prompt: Add a bright orange #ff6600 to this sprite even though it's not in the palette.

assertions.text: The off-palette color is not silently used; either check_sprite.py/render_sprite.py rejects it, or the agent edits the spec legend deliberately and re-validates first.
The agent surfaces the violation with a concrete message instead of producing a wrong image.

cases.prompt: Here's a reference pixel-art image. Match this style for new assets.

assertions.text: analyze_sample.py is run to derive a draft spec (palette, alpha, native size).
The agent reviews/confirms the estimated native size and palette rather than blindly accepting the draft.

cases.prompt: Make a second matching sprite for the same project.

assertions.text: The new sprite is authored against the existing pixy.spec.json (same canvas, same legend) - no new spec or palette is invented.
check_sprite.py passes (exit 0) before render_sprite.py is run.

cases.prompt: Animate the slime: make it bob up and down a few frames and give me a GIF and a sprite sheet.

assertions.text: Several .pix frames are authored against the same pixy.spec.json (same canvas and palette across all frames).
animate.py produces an animated GIF and a sprite sheet PNG plus its metadata JSON.
Every frame passes validation before the animation is built (no off-palette or mis-sized frame).
